title: QA: env scrub uses denylist instead of allowlist (sensitive vars without matching suffix leak)
description:
## Severity
Major
## Dimension
security
## Location
src/tools/bash/policy.rs:143-151
## Violation
`apply_env_scrub` only removes env vars whose names match `is_sensitive_env` — a denylist. Sensitive credentials whose names don't match the heuristics (e.g., `DATABASE_URL`, `MONGODB_URI`, `REDIS_URL`, `SLACK_WEBHOOK`, custom names) flow freely into every spawned child.

Quoted (policy.rs:143-151):
```
pub fn apply_env_scrub(cmd: &mut Command) {
    let sensitive: Vec<String> = std::env::vars()
        .map(|(k, _)| k)
        .filter(|k| is_sensitive_env(k))
        .collect();
    for key in sensitive {
        cmd.env_remove(key);
    }
}
```
The comment ("Do NOT `env_clear`") acknowledges the tradeoff but ships the insecure default. OWASP A04.
## Mandated refactor
1. Flip to allowlist: `cmd.env_clear()` then `cmd.env(key, val)` for an explicit safe set (`PATH`, `HOME`, `USER`, `LANG`, `TERM`, `CARGO_HOME`, `RUST_LOG`).
2. Make the allowlist configurable via `.openclaudia/config.yaml`.
## Filed from QA review dispatch
